results.question:

and ||x||_2^2 <= 29.697.

results.raw_response: {"reasoning": "The unconstrained minimum of the convex objective is found by setting gradient to zero: Q0 x + c0 = 0. Solving gives x = (13/18, -2/27, -19/54). This point satisfies all constraints (norm squared ~0.65 <= 33.971, constraint 1 value ~1.23 <= 18, constraint 2 value ~2.74 <= 111). Therefore it is the global optimum. The objective value at this point is 0.5 * c0^T x = -37/27 ≈ -1.37037.", "answer": -1.37037}

The unconstrained minimizer is x* = (8/11, 1/2, -3/11) with objective value 69/44 ≈ 1.56818. This point satisfies all constraints: constraint 1 evaluates to ≈4.66 ≤ 38, constraint 2 to ≈5.04 ≤ 25, and ||x*||^2 ≈0.85 ≤ 33.971. Since all constraints are inactive, the unconstrained optimum is the global solution.", "answer": 1.57}

Substituting into each constraint shows all are strictly satisfied. Since the problem is convex, this feasible point is globally optimal. The objective value is 0.5 * c_0^T x = -89/85 ≈ -1.0470588.", "answer": -1.04705882352941}

Minimize (1/2) x^T Q_0 x + c_0^T x
subject to x^T Q_i x + c_i^T x <= d_i for i=1..2,
and ||x||_2^2 <= 27.416.

Q_0 = [[6, 2, -6], [2, 6, -4], [-6, -4, 9]]
c_0 = [-1, 1, 0]

Q_2 = [[6.5, -2, 0], [-2, 9.5, -4], [0, -4, 2.5]], c_2 = [-4, -2, -2], d_2 = 56.5

Consider the convex quadratically constrained quadratic program over x in R^3:
Minimize (1/2) x^T Q_0 x + c_0^T x
subject to x^T Q_i x + c_i^T x <= d_i for i=1..2,
and ||x||_2^2 <= 29.697.

Q_0 = [[10, 2, 9], [2, 7, 2], [9, 2, 10]]
c_0 = [0, -1, 1]
Q_1 = [[6.5, 2, 3], [2, 8.5, -2], [3, -2, 6.5]], c_1 = [-3, 0, -1], d_1 = 45.0
Q_2 = [[5.5, 0, -2], [0, 6.5, 4], [-2, 4, 12.5]], c_2 = [-2, -2, 4], d_2 = 76.0

What is the minimum value of the objective?
results.raw_response: {"reasoning": "The problem is a convex QCQP in R^3. First, check if the unconstrained minimum of the objective is feasible. The objective is (1/2)x^T Q0 x + c0^T x with Q0 = [[10,2,9],[2,7,2],[9,2,10]], c0 = [0,-1,1]. The unconstrained minimizer is x* = -Q0^{-1} c0. Compute Q0^{-1}: det(Q0)=125, adj(Q0)=[[66,-2,-59],[-2,19,-2],[-59,-2,66]]. Then x* = (1/125)[57,21,-68]^T ≈ [0.456,0.168,-0.544]. Check constraints: norm squared ≈ 0.532 ≤ 29.697; x*^T Q1 x* + c1^T x* ≈ 1.87 ≤ 45; x*^T Q2 x* + c2^T x* ≈ 1.86 ≤ 76. All constraints are satisfied with large margins, so the unconstrained minimum is the global optimum. The optimal objective value is (1/2)c0^T x* = -89/250 = -0.356.", "answer": -0.356}
